hero_subtitle: SFTY helps VASPs and payment providers connect KYC, KYB, sanctions and risk data into a controlled onboarding workflow with audit-ready evidence and consistent decision logs.
trust_card_1_value: Identity checks connected to onboarding decisions
trust_card_2_value: Corporate verification, UBO review and entity risk
trust_card_3_value: Wallet and transaction monitoring in one workflow
trust_card_4_value: Message routing, completeness checks and exception handling
trust_card_5_value: Regulatory changes mapped to internal rule
trust_card_6_value: Decision logs and evidence for every review
problem_title: Onboarding slows when provider outputs disconnect
problem_description: Payment providers and VASPs face delays as KYC, KYB, sanctions, and screening results remain scattered across multiple systems. This fragmentation leads to high manual effort, inconsistent risk reviews and weaker audit trails for onboarding decisions.
problem_point_1_value: KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule outputs sit in separate tools
problem_point_2_value: Teams move data between dashboards, spreadsheets and internal notes
problem_point_3_value: Regulatory changes are hard to map into internal workflows
problem_point_4_value: Evidence and decision history are difficult to reconstruct later
solution_description: SFTY integrates KYC, KYB, sanctions, and screening provider results into a unified, risk-based onboarding workflow that centralizes decision logs and audit evidence for faster, more consistent reviews.
workflow_title: From onboarding checks to risk-based approval
workflow_description: SFTY connects KYC and KYB provider results with sanctions screening, risk rules, case review and structured approvals in a controlled onboarding workflow.
workflow_step_1_description: KYC, KYB, KYT and screening outputs enter SFTY
workflow_step_2_description: Risk rules route checks into the right workflow
workflow_step_3_description: A case opens with customer, wallet and provider context
workflow_step_4_description: Teams review, decide and record the outcome
workflow_step_5_description: Logs, decisions and reports stay ready for audit
why_sfty_title: Beyond fragmented onboarding. One compliance layer
why_sfty_description: SFTY connects KYC, KYB and screening outputs into structured workflows, risk-based reviews, evidence and decision logs without replacing existing onboarding providers.
why_sfty_card_1_description: Work above existing KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule tools
why_sfty_card_2_description: Turn disconnected outputs into structured reviews and cases
why_sfty_card_3_description: Preserve evidence, reviewer actions and decision history
why_sfty_card_4_description: Give compliance, risk and leadership one operating view
authority_description: SFTY supports VASPs onboarding crypto customers by connecting provider outputs, preserving audit evidence and enabling enterprise deployment with bank due diligence readiness.
authority_card_1_description: Connect KYC, KYB, KYT, Travel Rule and screening providers into one workflow
authority_card_2_description: Preserve provider outputs, reviewer notes, decisions and supporting records
authority_card_3_description: Prepare clearer compliance evidence for banks, partners and external review
authority_card_4_description: Support private environments, permissions, integrations and internal governance
ask_sfty_placeholder: How can SFTY support payment customer onboarding workflows
ask_sfty_chip_1_message: Explain how onboarding checks create audit-ready evidence
ask_sfty_chip_2_message: Show how risk-based routing manages sanctions and PEP screening
ask_sfty_chip_3_message: Describe provider orchestration for KYC and KYB verification
ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for product, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on provider orchestration, Travel Rule workflows, audit evidence and enterprise compliance operations.
faq_1_question: What is SFTY for payment onboarding teams?
faq_1_answer: SFTY provides payment onboarding teams a centralized compliance layer that connects KYC, KYB, sanctions, and risk data into structured workflows tailored for crypto-related customer due diligence.
faq_2_question: How does SFTY structure onboarding workflows?
faq_2_answer: SFTY organizes onboarding into risk-based workflows by connecting identity, company, sanctions, and adverse media checks with approval paths, alerts, case creation, decision logging and escalation.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY preserve onboarding evidence?
faq_3_answer: SFTY preserves audit-ready onboarding evidence by recording provider outputs, decision logs, reviewer actions and case history for clear traceability and review readiness.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY integrate with existing KYC providers?
faq_4_answer: Yes. SFTY integrates with your current KYC and KYB providers and orchestrates their outputs into one connected, policy-driven compliance workflow without disrupting existing tools.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace KYC or KYB service providers?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY does not replace KYC or KYB providers but sits above them to turn their outputs into structured onboarding reviews, decisions, cases and audit evidence.
final_description: Explore how SFTY connects KYC, KYB, sanctions and risk data into audit-ready onboarding workflows and decision records.
footer_description: Supervisory and compliance control layer for digital asset markets, businesses and regulated institutions.
footer_contact_title: For supervisory, compliance and deployment discussions.
footer_legal_notice: This page is for informational purposes only and does not constitute legal, regulatory or compliance advice. SFTY deployment depends on jurisdiction, client requirements and applicable laws.
